Article Title: Run-out controversy! 'Never do what Bangladesh captain did': Ex-India star backs Salman Ali Agha, slams Mehidy Hasan Miraz
Article Text:
AFP Photo

EXCLUSIVE: Rahul Dravid on iconic Eden Gardens win against Australia in 2001

Poll Do you think the run-out incident was unsportsmanlike? Yes No

Former India cricketer Mohammad Kaif slammed Mehidy Hasan Miraz after a controversial run-out involving Salman Ali Agha during the second ODI between Bangladesh and Pakistan at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium in Dhaka on Friday.The unusual dismissal sparked a major debate on social media, with Bangladesh captain Mehidy Hasan Miraz facing criticism for what many termed an unsportsmanlike act. Former India batter Kaif was among those who strongly condemned the incident.Slamming the act, Kaif said: "This is so not done. No desperation of getting wickets can justify this run out. That too from a captain. Youngsters please remember, even if the World Cup final is on the line never do what the Bangladesh skipper did.Sports without fair play is no sports."The incident took place in the 39th over of Pakistan’s innings when the visitors were rebuilding after losing three early wickets. Agha and Mohammad Rizwan had steadied the innings with a 109-run partnership for the fourth wicket.On the fourth ball of the over, bowled by Miraz, Rizwan gently pushed the delivery down the ground. The ball rolled towards Agha, who had backed up and was standing outside the crease at the non-striker’s end.As the ball struck his pads, Agha instinctively bent down, picked it up and attempted to hand it back. Miraz, however, quickly grabbed the ball and dislodged the bails with Agha still outside the crease before appealing for a run-out.The on-field umpire referred the decision to the third umpire after the appeal was made, and the replay confirmed the dismissal in Bangladesh’s favour.The decision left Agha visibly frustrated as he walked back to the pavilion, with television visuals showing the Pakistan batter expressing his displeasure after the call.The incident also triggered brief exchanges between players from both teams on the field. Rizwan was seen having a discussion with the Bangladesh players, while Litton Das defended the appeal made by Miraz.The rare run-out quickly became the biggest talking point of the match, dividing opinion among fans online. While many supported Agha and criticised the spirit of the dismissal, others argued that the Pakistan batter should have ensured he returned to his crease.Meanwhile, Pakistan were eventually bowled out for 274 in 47.3 overs, with Maaz Sadaqat top-scoring with 75. Having already lost the first ODI, Pakistan needed a victory in the second match to keep the series alive.
Article Summary: Mohammad Kaif slams Mehidy Hasan Miraz after controversial run-out. Incident took place during second ODI between Bangladesh and Pakistan. Bangladesh captain Miraz appealed for the run out of Salman Ali Agha. Incident sparked a major debate on social media. Former India batter Kaif among those who strongly condemned the act.

Article Title: 'That sensational partnership changed everything': John Wright on VVS Laxman–Rahul Dravid epic
Article Text:
VVS Laxman and Rahul Dravid (AFP Photo)

When you look back at that Eden Gardens Test, 25 years later, what stands out the most for you?

EXCLUSIVE: Rahul Dravid on iconic Eden Gardens win against Australia in 2001

What were the circumstances around the Indian team before that match?

How did the absence of

Anil Kumble

shape the team's plans?

The turning point of the Test was VVS Laxman’s promotion up the order. How did that decision come about?

What made VVS Laxman’s innings so remarkable?

Apart from his batting, how else did Laxman contribute to the team's dynamics and development?

How crucial was Rahul Dravid’s contribution?

Harbhajan Singh’s bowling on the final day was legendary. Who were the other unsung heroes?

What do you recall about that manic final day?

How important was that win for the team?

In the cauldron that was the Eden Gardens in March 2001, John Wright — the first foreigner to coach India — witnessed the ebb and flow of the Test, with all its drama, emotion and sheer improbability. Twenty-five years later, the New Zealander tells TOI from Christchurch, of the pressure, the tactical decisions and the far-reaching impact of beating Australia.I can't believe it's 25 years already! I had never seen crowds like that before. It was packed to the rafters. Things looked bleak for us on Day 3. But then came that incredible turnaround over the next two days, thanks largely to that sensational partnership between VVS Laxman and Rahul Dravid . It eventually culminated in Harbhajan Singh ’s bowling heroics on the final day. The pressure on us was enormous.Australia were the world champions and they had been winning everything.It was still my early days as the first foreign coach of the Indian team, and Sourav Ganguly was also a relatively new captain. The odds were stacked against us. Before the series we had a preparatory camp in Chennai with our physio Andrew Leipus. The big question we kept asking ourselves was simple: how do we beat Australia? I think we prepared extremely well for that challenge.Our most experienced spinner, Anil Kumble, was injured and unavailable. The selectors had identified a young off-spinner named Harbhajan Singh, and this series ended up defining his career. Looking back, I would rank this series with the 2004 series win against Pakistan in terms of its importance.After the first innings we were asked to follow on, but Laxman had already batted beautifully and scored 59. We were also trailing 1–0 in the series, so in a sense we had nothing to lose. I remember listening to Ian Chappell on television commentary. He’s one commentator whose views I value greatly. Ian suggested that India should promote Laxman up the order because he was in terrific form. What he said made a lot of sense.We wanted someone who could play aggressively and put pressure on Glenn McGrath and Shane Warne. Sourav and I sat down after the first innings and agreed that promoting VVS (Laxman) was the best way forward. I’ve always believed that the captain is the central figure in a team. The coach is more on the periphery. But in this case, our collective decision worked perfectly.What fascinated me the most was the way he played Shane Warne. He often played him against the spin and also got to the pitch of the ball so effortlessly. It was a masterclass on how to play spin. After tea that day he played two glorious off drives on the up: one against McGrath and the other off Jason Gillespie. I remember thinking: how on earth does he play like that? It was so regal.Laxman was the bridge between the younger players and the seniors in the squad. If I remember correctly, he was sharing a room with Zaheer Khan . We had a policy at the time where a bowler would room with a batter. Laxman spent a lot of time giving Zak (Zaheer) advice on his batting. During my early stint as coach, Sourav and I focused on three key areas: improving close catching, sharpening running between the wickets, and raising overall fitness levels. We also wanted the lower order to contribute more so that we wouldn’t collapse after being 200/5. The only thing missing in that squad was a genuine all-rounder.Absolutely. Rahul deserves enormous credit. He was pushed down to No.6 in the second innings. It was a demotion, but he was a sport. His 180 was monumental. Laxman and Dravid were a terrific pair. They were also involved in another remarkable partnership against Australia in Adelaide in 2003. Rahul was in an incredibly productive phase of his career then. Whether it was Headingley in 2002, Adelaide in 2003 or Lahore in 2004. He was truly our rock.Harbhajan’s performance, including that famous hat-trick, is rightly remembered. But we shouldn’t forget Sachin Tendulkar’s spell either. He picked up three crucial wickets. The pitch was gripping on the final day, and it was a masterstroke from Sourav to give Sachin the ball. I also remember SS Das taking two outstanding catches. There were contributions from everyone in that match.It was incredibly tense. I watched from a distance as the drama unfolded. Eden Gardens had turned into a cauldron. The ebb and flow made it riveting. I’ve always believed India has the best cricket fans in the world and they deserved every bit of the entertainment they got.The victory at Eden Gardens gave us enormous confidence heading into the third Test in Chennai, which we also went on to win. It also extended my tenure as Team India’s coach. I stayed with the team for another four years. If we had lost that series, I would have packed my bags and returned home. At the time, everything was happening so quickly that you didn’t fully absorb it. Looking back, I think that victory injected self-belief in the team. It also paved the way for the overseas successes that followed.
Article Summary: John Wright was the first foreign coach of the Indian team in 2001. The Kiwi recalls the pressure, tactical decisions and the far-reaching impact of beating Australia. The turning point of the Test was VVS Laxman’s promotion up the order.

Article Title: 'Rascal': Ex-Australia wicketkeeper's bold remark on Abhishek Sharma
Article Text:
Abhishek Sharma (ICC Photo)

Suryakumar Yadav receives grand homecoming after India’s T20 World Cup win

Poll Did Abhishek Sharma live up to the expectations placed on him before the T20 World Cup? Yes, by performing in key moments No, he fell short overall

NEW DELHI: India's star opener Abhishek Sharma found himself at the centre of heavy criticism during the T20 World Cup 2026. The left-hander endured a disastrous start to the tournament, failing to open his account in the first three matches as he registered three consecutive ducks.In the lead-up to the final, Abhishek managed just 89 runs in seven matches, including those three ducks in the group stage. He briefly returned among the runs but scored only 15 against South Africa in the fourth match. He then rediscovered his touch with a quickfire 55 against Zimbabwe in the fifth game. However, his form dipped again as he could only manage 10 against West Indies and nine in the semifinal against England.Despite the inconsistent run, the team management continued to back him, with coach Gautam Gambhir and captain Suryakumar Yadav showing full faith in the young opener.In the final, Abhishek turned the heat on the New Zealand bowlers, smashing an 18-ball fifty as India piled up a massive 255/5. India then restricted New Zealand to 159 to successfully defend their T20 World Cup title.Former Australia wicketkeeper Brad Haddin was full of praise for Abhishek, playfully calling him a "rascal"."I'm a huge fan of Abhishek, and there was a lot of talk about his position throughout the tournament. Got a few ducks in a row in the pool games, and all of a sudden, you think he is under pressure, but there are the guys you want in the big moments, because they are rascals and they walk into the fire. That is what Abhishek does, he puts pressure on the opposition, and the way that he went out in that final set up the game," the former wicketkeeper said on the Willow Talk Cricket Podcast.Haddin also praised New Zealand for reaching yet another final but pointed out their repeated struggles at the last hurdle."New Zealand have done a good job again to get to a final. I thought New Zealand were really red hot for the final. The way Finn Allen and Tim Seifert were batting. I thought they were in one of those tournament modes where everything is going their way. But the last hurdle [they failed to cross]. The classier team won, didn't it?" he added.Abhishek finished the tournament with 141 runs at a strike rate of 158.42.
Article Summary: India's star opener Abhishek Sharma found himself at the centre of heavy criticism during the T20 World Cup 2026. The left-hander endured a disastrous start to the tournament, failing to open his account in the first three matches as he registered three consecutive ducks. In the lead-up to the final, he managed just 89 runs in seven matches, including those three ducks in the group stage.

Article Title: 'When captain Hardik Pandya needs a shoulder, Rohit Sharma is there': Harbhajan Singh
Article Text:
Hardik Pandya and Rohit Sharma (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

EXCLUSIVE: Rahul Dravid on iconic Eden Gardens win against Australia in 2001

Poll Should Rohit Sharma remain an active player on the field instead of being used as an Impact Player? Yes, he should play actively. No, using him as an Impact Player is better.

NEW DELHI: Rohit Sharma , who led Mumbai Indians (MI) to five Indian Premier League (IPL) titles, is set to return to competitive cricket in the upcoming IPL season starting March 28. Legendary Indian spinner and former MI player Harbhajan Singh believes Rohit should remain actively involved on the field rather than being used as an Impact Player.The Impact Player rule, introduced in the IPL in 2023, allows teams to substitute one member of the playing XI with one of five designated substitutes at any point during a match.This tactical 12th player can bat and bowl their full quota of overs, significantly enhancing team strategy, increasing scoring rates and enabling specialised batting or bowling reinforcements.Speaking on JioStar, former India spinner Harbhajan urged the Mumbai Indians management to keep Rohit on the field.He said, "Rohit Sharma has been used as the 12th man who just bats, but I feel that the kind of leader he has been, a player like him should be on the field."The 38-year-old featured in most matches as an Impact Player last season, when Mumbai Indians finished fourth in the points table.Harbhajan also believes MI captain Hardik Pandya can benefit from Rohit Sharma’s experience while making crucial decisions during matches."In tough matches, when you need to take certain calls, when a captain sometimes needs a shoulder to lean on, Rohit Sharma can do that for Hardik Pandya," said Harbhajan.In the last IPL season, Rohit scored 418 runs in 15 matches for the Mumbai Indians, averaging 29.86 with a strike rate of 149.29, including a highest score of 81.He achieved this after a slow start to the season, finishing with four 50+ scores and surpassing 7,000 career IPL runs during the eliminator against the Gujarat Titans.
Article Summary: Rohit Sharma set to return to competitive cricket in the upcoming IPL season. Impact Player rule allows teams to substitute one member of the playing XI with one of five designated substitutes at any point during a match. Legendary Indian spinner Harbhajan Singh believes Rohit should remain actively involved on the field.

Article Title: 'No one can tell MS Dhoni to sit out': Ex-CSK star's bold claim on dressing room reality
Article Text:
Mahendra Singh Dhoni (PTI Photo/Kunal Patil)

Spectators are priority! Here's what IPL must learn from the SA20 experience

Former India batter Subramaniam Badrinath believes it would be extremely difficult for the Chennai Super Kings team management to leave MS Dhoni out of the playing XI in the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League.Dhoni is set to return for Chennai Super Kings in the 2026 campaign, with franchise CEO Kasi Viswanath already confirming that the veteran will be available for the entire tournament. However, the exact role he will play during matches is yet to be finalised. According to Viswanath, the decision will be taken by the team management, who will decide whether Dhoni features as the wicketkeeper or is used as an impact player depending on the match situation.The 44-year-old had a relatively quiet season in IPL 2025 season, scoring 196 runs from 14 matches. Batting mostly in the lower order, Dhoni averaged 24.50 and maintained a strike rate of 135.17 while often coming in during the closing stages of the innings to provide quick runs.Despite questions around his role, Badrinath feels the management would find it almost impossible to bench the legendary captain given the stature he holds within the franchise. Speaking on his YouTube channel, the former CSK batter suggested that even head coach Stephen Fleming may not be in a position to make such a call."I don't know if it is an administrative decision where he (CSK CEO) said that he (Dhoni) is ready to play all matches.He said that he would support the cricketing decision and stop. Now, will Fleming go and tell Dhoni that you don't play this match, we are dropping you. I don't think Fleming has a chance at all. How will he go and tell Dhoni that you are dropped? I literally feel that it is not possible," Badrinath said on his YouTube channel.Badrinath further emphasised the level of respect Dhoni commands inside the CSK dressing room. According to him, it would be extremely hard for any member of the coaching staff to even suggest that the former India captain should sit out or be used only as a substitute."I don't think any support staff in the world, be it even Fleming, can tell Dhoni you are an impact player, or you don't play this match. There is no chance. We can take it that he is going to play all the matches," he added.
Article Summary: MS Dhoni is set to return for Chennai Super Kings in the 2026 IPL. Former India batter Subramaniam Badrinath believes it would be extremely difficult for the team management to leave Dhoni out of the playing XI. The 44-year-old had a relatively quiet season, scoring 196 runs from 14 matches.

Article Title: 'Rohit Sharma can’t watch 20 overs from the dugout': Ex-India cricketer’s blunt take on MI’s Impact Player strategy

Article Title: Paul Skenes: "Spoon fed": Paul Skenes breaks silence after Bubba Chandler questions Pirates ace’s rapid rise to MLB spotlight
Article Text:
"Spoon fed" Paul Skenes breaks silence after Bubba Chandler questions Pirates ace’s rapid rise to MLB spotlight (Image via Getty Images)

Pittsburgh Pirates pitcher Paul Skenes responds after teammate Bubba Chandler jokes he was “spoon fed” in fast MLB rise

Paul Skenes and Bubba Chandler created a funny moment that quickly caught fans’ attention. The Pittsburgh Pirates teammates were joking about Skenes’ fast rise in Major League Baseball, and Chandler did not hold back during the interview. Chandler teased the Pirates ace by saying Skenes was “spoon fed” because he reached the major leagues so quickly. His comment surprised many fans, but it was clearly meant as friendly locker room banter. When Skenes later heard about the remark, he gave a calm but funny reply.The moment showed the light side of the Pirates' clubhouse. Even though Skenes is now one of the most talked about pitchers in baseball, his teammates still enjoy pulling his leg.The young pitcher has become a huge name since making his MLB debut with the Pittsburgh Pirates in 2024. His performances quickly turned him into one of the league’s most exciting arms. But as Chandler’s joke showed, inside the clubhouse Skenes is still just another teammate who can be teased like everyone else.During the interview, Bubba Chandler explained how Pirates players often joke with Paul Skenes about how quickly he reached the major leagues.Chandler said the team enjoys reminding Skenes about being the first overall pick in the MLB draft and how little time he spent in the minor leagues.“We always bust his chops on, you know, he’s spoon-fed,” Chandler said. “One, first overall pick, spent 14 minutes in the minor leagues. Like, ‘You just spoon-fed dude, like you didn’t have to grind. You didn’t play in the FCL as I did.’” The comment quickly became a talking point. When Skenes later heard what his teammate said, he gave a playful response of his own.“I don’t know what Bubba is talking about,” Skenes said. “I’m the one who needs to check my ego? Tell him to look in the mirror. Yeah I’m a grinder, contrary to what people think, I’m a grinder.” The moment showed the easygoing relationship between the two Pirates pitchers. While Skenes has already reached superstar status, he still enjoys the jokes inside the team.After a strong season with Pittsburgh, Skenes recently represented Team USA in the World Baseball Classic, adding another big milestone to his young career. Meanwhile, Chandler has been focusing on preparing for the upcoming season with the Pirates during Spring Training.In a recent Spring Training game against the Atlanta Braves, Chandler delivered an impressive outing. During the game, he threw 18 straight fastballs, all called by Pirates catcher Henry Davis. The idea was simple. Davis wanted to test Chandler’s control and see how accurate his fastball could be.“Plans aren’t a thing,” Chandler said. “Throw the first pitch, and every plan goes out the window. Whatever Henry called, I threw. It felt good coming out.” Chandler ended the game with strong numbers. He threw 66 pitches, with 47 strikes, and created 15 swings and misses. Out of those 66 pitches, 49 were four-seam fastballs.It was a strong step forward for Chandler after earlier struggles in Grapefruit League games. For the Pirates, moments like these show both competition and friendship inside the team. Players push each other to improve, but they also keep the mood light with jokes like the one Chandler made about Skenes.
Article Summary: Pittsburgh Pirates pitcher Paul Skenes responds after teammate Bubba Chandler jokes he was “spoon fed” in fast MLB rise. Chandler said the team enjoys reminding S Kenes about being the first overall pick in the MLB draft and how little time he spent in the minor leagues. The moment showed the light side of the Pirates' clubhouse.

Article Title: Nick Kyrgios love life: From Jannik Sinner's ex-girlfriend Anna Kalinskaya to Costeen Hatzi, inside the relationships of the tennis star
Article Text:
All anout Nick Kyrgios' relationships.Image via: X/ Getty

Dating Anna Kalinskaya and Australian tennis star Ajla Tomljanović

Nick Kyrgios has never done anything quietly. From the volleys he hits to the opinions he shares, everything about the Australian tennis star comes with volume. On the court, he has produced some of the most exciting shots the sport has witnessed in recent years. Off it, he has been just as interesting and just as unpredictable. A Wimbledon finalist in 2022 and a former top-20 player, Kyrgios has spent the better part of a decade as one of tennis's most talked-about personalities and that conversation has never been limited to what happens between the lines.His personal life has always drawn attention, partly because Kyrgios has never been particularly guarded about it. His candid social media presence and outspoken nature have meant that his relationships often play out at least partially in public view.Here is a look at the relationships that have shaped his story off the court.Nick Kyrgios' first well-known relationship was with fellow Australian tennis player Ajla Tomljanović. The two dated between 2015 and 2017. It was the kind of pairing that made natural sense. Two young Australians navigating the same world at the same time. They even partnered up for mixed doubles at the 2016 Australian Open, which gave fans a rare glimpse of them on the same side of the net.When they eventually went their separate ways, both continued building careers that would take them to very different places in the sport.After his relationship with Tomljanović ended, Kyrgios was linked to Russian tennis player Anna Kalinskaya, who later went out with Jannik Sinner. More publicly, he went on to date Australian influencer Chiara Passari from around 2020 to 2021. The relationship attracted a lot of attention and not always for the right reasons. Their breakup sparked a wave of headlines and legal proceedings that kept both names in the news long after the relationship itself had ended.The most significant relationship of Kyrgios' life so far began in one of the more unlikely ways you will come across. In late 2021, he came across Australian social media influencer Costeen Hatzi's Instagram page, where she had listed a mirror for sale. He sent her a message. That was how it started.What followed quickly became one of the most talked-about relationships in Australian tennis. Hatzi became a regular presence at his matches and was in the stands for some of the biggest moments of his career, including his remarkable run to the Wimbledon final in 2022. Their relationship also featured in the Netflix tennis series Break Point, which gave fans a genuinely personal look at Kyrgios away from the noise and the controversies. In 2025, Nick broke silence on their breakup and said he wished her well. He is now rumored to be dating Melbourne-based marketing manager Alexia Alessi.
Article Summary: Nick Kyrgios is one of the most talked-about tennis players in the world. His personal life has always drawn attention, partly because he has never been particularly guarded about it. He has dated Anna Kalinskaya, Ajla Tomljanović, Chiara Passari and Costeen Hatzi.

Article Title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding rumors spark royal level excitement as fans compare event to Princess Diana
Article Text:
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce (Image Via Getty)

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding plans spark global excitement as fans compare event to royal wedding

Swift has also spoken about the relationship in recent interviews. During a conversation with BBC Radio 2, she responded to suggestions that marriage might slow down her career. Swift said the idea was “shockingly offensive,” making it clear that she has no plans to step away from music.

She added that she and Kelce share a strong connection because both are highly competitive and passionate about their work. According to Swift, what Kelce calls “practice” in football feels similar to what she calls “rehearsal” before performing.

For now, the couple’s wedding plans continue to draw attention across the world. With Swift’s global fan base and Kelce’s popularity in the NFL, many people believe the ceremony could become one of the biggest celebrity events of the year.

The relationship between Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce has been one of the most talked about stories in sports and entertainment. Now the couple is moving closer to a major moment. Reports say Swift and Kelce are planning a wedding in mid June 2026, and the news has already created huge excitement among fans.According to reports, the ceremony is expected to take place at Taylor Swift’s Ocean House estate in Watch Hill, Rhode Island. The location has already seen increased hotel bookings as fans and media prepare for what many people believe could become one of the most watched celebrity weddings in recent years.Some commentators have even compared the global interest in the event to the famous 1981 wedding of Princess Diana and King Charles III.Interest in the wedding began growing after Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift announced their engagement in August 2025. Since then, the couple’s relationship has remained a major topic across sports and entertainment media.The timing of the wedding appears carefully planned. Reports suggest the couple chose a mid June ceremony so they could celebrate before the Kansas City Chiefs begin NFL training camp in late July.Kelce also recently made an important career decision. The star tight end signed a one year contract extension worth about $12 million to return for his 14th season with the Chiefs. According to people close to the situation, the couple worked together to find a date that would fit around Kelce’s football schedule.Media personality Justin Sylvester recently spoke about the public interest surrounding the wedding. While discussing the topic, he compared the level of attention to one of the most famous weddings in history.Sylvester said the excitement around Swift and Kelce reminds him of the 1981 wedding of Princess Diana and King Charles, noting how both partners bring huge global audiences.While fans focus on the upcoming wedding, both Swift and Kelce continue to talk openly about how they support each other’s careers.During an appearance on The Pat McAfee Show on March 10, Kelce explained that Swift’s dedication to her work inspired him to keep playing football instead of retiring.Kelce said seeing Swift’s commitment made him realize he still had the energy to compete. He explained that watching her continue creating music and performing made him feel like he still had “juice left” to play the sport he loves.
Article Summary: The wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce is set to take place in June 2026. The wedding is expected to be one of the most watched events of the year. The couple have already signed a one-year contract extension with the Kansas City Chiefs.

Article Title: Iga Swiatek vs Elina Svitolina Indian Wells 2026 quarterfinal sparks outrage as Tennis Channel coverage leaves fans furious
Article Text:
Iga Swiatek vs Elina Svitolina (Image Via Getty)

Elina Svitolina shocks Iga Swiatek at Indian Wells as Tennis Channel coverage sparks backlash

Tennis fans expected a big moment when Iga Swiatek faced Elina Svitolina in the quarterfinals of the BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells 2026. The match had everything people wanted from a top clash. But many viewers in the United States say they could not fully watch the action because of issues with the Tennis Channel broadcast.The problem quickly became a big talking point online. While the match delivered drama on court, fans complained that the television coverage did not properly show key moments. Many viewers went to social media to express their anger, saying a major quarterfinal between two top players deserved better coverage.On the court, Elina Svitolina delivered one of the most impressive wins of the tournament. The Ukrainian defeated Iga Swiatek 6–2, 4–6, 6–4 in a match that lasted two hours and nine minutes. With this victory, Svitolina reached the Indian Wells semifinals and improved her strong start to the 2026 season.The win was important for Svitolina because she had previously beaten Swiatek only once on the WTA Tour, which came at Wimbledon in 2023.After this result, her record in three set matches this season improved to 6–0, showing how strong she has been in long battles. Speaking after the match, Svitolina explained that her plan was to stay aggressive instead of waiting for mistakes.She said, “There is no champion who is waiting for mistakes. You really have to try to set yourself up in a good position to attack. I was feeling good and trying to open up the court and take my chances because Iga is such an aggressive player.” The turning point came late in the final set. With the score at 5–4, Svitolina broke Swiatek’s serve and then calmly served out the match to complete the upset.However, while the players delivered a thrilling contest, many fans said the Tennis Channel broadcast did not show the match properly. Viewers complained that studio discussion and other segments interrupted coverage of the quarterfinal.One fan wrote on social media, “Tennis Channel choosing to show Wertheim and Eubanks talking about men’s matches instead of showing the WTA quarterfinal is why sports media is so frustrating.”Another viewer questioned the network’s broadcast choice and wrote, “This feed should be exclusively Elina Svitolina vs Iga Swiatek. What is going on?” Some fans were even more direct. One post said, “Tennis Channel abandoning a top matchup at 2–2 in the final set so they can do desk talk, so unserious.”The criticism also reminded fans of earlier broadcast complaints. In the past, viewers have raised similar concerns during events like the Italian Open and Madrid Open, where matches were interrupted by ads or delayed feeds.For now, the result on the court is clear. Elina Svitolina moves into the Indian Wells semifinals, while the broadcast debate has created another discussion among tennis fans watching from home.
Article Summary: Elina Svitolina defeated Iga Swiatek 6–2, 4–6, 6–4 in the quarterfinals of the BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells 2026. Many viewers in the United States say they could not fully watch the action because of issues with the Tennis Channel broadcast.

Article Title: Stefon Diggs Cardi B Breakup: Stefon Diggs–Cardi B breakup takes a new turn as Offset is reportedly obsessing over his ex
Article Text:
Image - X

One Night with Steiny

After Cardi B’s rumored split from Stefon Diggs, Celina Powell says Offset is still obsessed with his ex



"All this man wants to do,"

"is go gamble, and then come back and cuddle and talk about Cardi B all day." I

any

"Yeah, it’s sunny, but you know what? I bet it’s raining where Cardi’s at."

Since the famous breakup of Stefon Diggs and Cardi B , people have been seeing a lot of videos and rumors about her reuniting with Offset. Initially, it was just wishful thinking on the fans’ part, but now there seems to be some reality to it.Because Celina Powell is back at it again. The self-proclaimed "Black Widow" of the internet just dropped a bombshell that has everyone looking at Offset a little bit differently. In a recent appearance on thepodcast, Celina didn't just spill the tea, she dumped the whole pot.According to Celina, the tough-guy persona we see in music videos is all for show. She claimed that behind closed doors, Offset isn't exactly the "Ric Flair" of the rap game.Instead, she alleges that he is actually a "world-class cuddler" who has one specific topic of conversation on his mind 24/7: his ex-wife, Cardi B.Celina told Steiny that their private time wasn't exactly what people would expect.she claimed with a smirk,t’s a wild image to wrap your head around, one of the biggest rappers in the world allegedly pinned up against someone, venting about his ex like it’s a therapy session.The details got even weirder. Celina described a pattern where literallytopic would circle back to the "Bodak Yellow" rapper. She joked that if she asked about the weather, Offset would probably respond with,If these claims are true, it paints a picture of a man who is deeply, perhaps obsessively, still hung up on the mother of his children. While the internet has known for a while that their breakup was messy, this adds a whole new level of "down bad" to the situation.Of course, we have to take this with a grain of salt. This "expose" comes right after Celina started a massive "crash out" campaign against Offset in early 2026. She claims he owes her $15,000 and has even leaked videos of him sleeping to prove they were together.Between the alleged FaceTime threats to NFL star Stefon Diggs and these new "pillow talk" stories, Celina is clearly on a mission to dismantle Offset’s reputation. Whether he’s actually a "softie" who just wants to snuggle and talk about his ex, or if this is just Celina’s latest play for clout, one thing is for sure: the drama is far from over.
Article Summary: Celina Powell claims Offset is obsessed with his ex-wife, Cardi B. She claims he is a "world-class cuddler" who has one topic of conversation on his mind 24/7. This "expose" comes right after Celina started a massive "crash out" campaign against Offset.

Article Title: Serena Williams shares powerful body positivity message after transformation as 2026 tennis comeback rumors swirl
Article Text:
Serena Williams shares powerful body positivity message after transformation as 2026 tennis comeback rumors swirl (Image via Getty)

Serena Williams opens up about body image struggles and how she learned to ignore criticism



Serena Williams is once again getting people talking, but this time it is not about a tennis match. The 23-time Grand Slam champion recently shared a heartfelt message on Instagram about accepting her body and feeling comfortable in her own skin. The post came after fans noticed her physical transformation following a weight-loss journey that included changes in diet, exercise, and the use of GLP-1 medication.In the photos, Serena Williams stood outdoors near a pool, surrounded by trees. She wore an olive-green one-piece swimsuit, a soft robe, and tan slippers. Along with the photos, she shared a simple but powerful message about self-acceptance. Serena Williams wrote on Instagram, “I am not perfect….but I’m perfectly me.Take out time today to celebrate being perfectly you! 💋.”Serena Williams has spent decades in the public eye, winning 73 WTA singles titles and becoming one of the most successful players in tennis history. But despite all the victories, she has spoken honestly about a personal challenge that followed her throughout her career: body image.Early in her tennis journey, Serena Williams often looked different from many other players on the tour.Her powerful and muscular build stood out at a time when many female athletes were expected to look thinner. The difference sometimes brought criticism and unwanted comments.Speaking to Net-a-Porter in December, Serena Williams explained how those early years affected her mentally. She said, “It was hard because when I was playing in the beginning – the first 15 years – my body was different. I had big boobs; I had a big butt. Every athlete was like super flat, super thin and beautiful, but in a different way. And I didn’t understand as an athlete how to deal with that. It does affect you mentally. Absolutely.”Over time, Serena Williams says she learned to focus on her own mindset rather than outside opinions. The tennis legend explained that trying to change what others think is not worth the emotional stress.She told Net-a-Porter, “I don’t hear the noise. Everyone is entitled to their opinion. How am I going to sit here and change someone’s thought? If you don’t like me, you don’t have to. I’m not going to let anyone bring me down. I put enough stress on myself. The last thing I’m going to allow is someone else to do that.”Meanwhile, Serena Williams has also sparked fresh discussion in the tennis world for another reason. According to the International Tennis Integrity Agency testing pool list, her name appeared again in October. Under tennis rules, any player planning a comeback must spend six months in the testing pool before returning to competition.Because of that process, Serena Williams became eligible to return to professional tennis after February 22, 2026, though she has not officially announced any comeback plans.For now, her recent message on Instagram shows that Serena Williams is focusing on confidence, self-respect, and celebrating individuality, lessons that go far beyond tennis.
Article Summary: Serena Williams shared a powerful message about self-acceptance on Instagram. The 23-time Grand Slam champion recently underwent a weight-loss journey. Serena Williams has also sparked fresh discussion in the tennis world for another reason. According to the International Tennis Integrity Agency testing pool list, she is eligible to return to professional tennis after February 22, 2026.

Article Title: NHL trade update: Colorado Avalanche sign defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to entry-level contract
Article Text:
The Colorado Avalanche have signed defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to a two-year entry-level contract. (Image via Twitter/X)

Colorado Avalanche sign defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to an entry-level contract

The Colorado Avalanche have signed defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to a two-year contract after the Swedish blueliner completed his collegiate career at Bowling Green State University. This was announced by the club on Friday. As per reports, the deal will kick in for the start of the 2026-27 season, and Stjernberg will play on an AHL contract for the Avalanche’s minor-league affiliate, Colorado Eagles, for the remainder of this season. The exact financial details of the deal have not yet been released.According to reports, Stjernberg has spent the last three seasons with Bowling Green, where he also served as an alternate captain in the 2025-26 season.In 25 games this year, the Swedish defenseman recorded six goals and four assists for 10 points. Over his three-year collegiate career, he totaled 14 goals and 23 assists for 37 points in 86 games.The Colorado Avalanche have recently signed the Swedish defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to a two-year entry-level contract. The deal begins in the 2026-27 season, while Stjernberg will join the AHL’s Colorado Eagles for the rest of the current campaign on a separate contract.The club announced the same on Friday, and also noted that Stjernberg’s NHL deal will kick in next season, giving the organization a chance to get an early look at him in the AHL before he officially becomes part of their NHL roster picture. However, after the announcement of the signing of the defenseman, the financial terms were not disclosed, and are still in line with team policy.Stjernberg is considered a coming off player, who has played for a three-year career at Bowling Green State University. As for his performance, in 25 games this season serving as an alternate captain, the In addition to this, Swedish national also had six goals and four assists for 10 points. Last season, he put up four goals and 12 assists for 16 points in 30 games.Over 86 NCAA games, the right-shot defender totaled 14 goals and 23 assists for 37 points. Before heading to college, he played one season in the USHL with the Des Moines Buccaneers, putting up 20 points (eight goals, 12 assists) in 62 games.In the 2023-24 season, his first for Bowling Green, he registered four goals and seven assists for 11 points in 31 games. In his collegiate career, Stjernberg compiled 14 goals and 23 assists for 37 points in 86 games played. His strong hold on the game and impressive career stats made him join the league with the Colorado Avalanche.
Article Summary: Colorado Avalanche sign defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to an entry-level contract. The Swedish blueliner completed his collegiate career at Bowling Green State University. The deal will kick in for the start of the 2026-27 season. The financial details of the deal have not yet been released. In 25 games this year, the Swedish defenseman recorded six goals and four assists for 10 points.

Article Title: Donald Trump proposes unexpected Jake Paul vs Khabib Nurmagomedov fight despite retired UFC legend standing firm on retirement
Article Text:
Donald Trump proposes unexpected Jake Paul vs Khabib Nurmagomedov fight despite retired UFC legend standing firm on retirement (Image via Getty)

Donald Trump tells Jake Paul he should fight retired UFC champion Khabib Nurmagomedov



Donald Trump has thrown out a surprising idea for a future fight. He told Jake Paul that he should try to bring UFC legend Khabib Nurmagomedov out of retirement. Khabib Nurmagomedov walked away from MMA in 2020 as the UFC lightweight champion. He finished his career with a perfect 29 wins and no losses. Since then, he has stayed firm on his decision to retire. Even huge offers have not changed his mind. One of those offers was reportedly worth $100 million to fight boxing icon Floyd Mayweather.Still, Donald Trump believes a fight between Jake Paul and Khabib Nurmagomedov would be interesting to watch. Jake Paul has already built a reputation by fighting former MMA stars in boxing matches.Over the past few years he has beaten fighters like Anderson Silva, Nate Diaz, and Ben Askren. He also knocked out Tyron Woodley in their rematch. Now, Donald Trump thinks the next name on that list should be Khabib Nurmagomedov.The unexpected suggestion came during a conversation between Donald Trump and Jake Paul at a rally event supporting Trump. According to Jake Paul, they later recorded a podcast-style talk which he described as “controversial.”During the conversation, they started talking about combat sports. Donald Trump has often spoken about his interest in boxing and MMA.Jake Paul was asked about his next opponent. He explained that he wants to stay close to the cruiserweight division in boxing, or possibly try MMA in the future. While discussing possible fights, he mentioned several big names. These included Oleksandr Usyk, Francis Ngannou, and Ryan Garcia.Then Jake Paul asked Donald Trump who he would like to see him fight.Trump responded directly:“I would probably say Khabib. When people say he was the best, he really was great.”Jake Paul did not reject the idea. Instead, he said he would be open to it, though another possible opponent being discussed is Tommy Fury.Paul also gave an update about his health. He said his jaw is still recovering from his last fight with Anthony Joshua, a bout he said he even watched again on Netflix while traveling with Donald Trump on Air Force One.According to Jake Paul, doctors have told him that full-contact sparring may not be possible until later in the year.However, he sounded confident about the recovery. Jake Paul said, “My jaw is healing and it might actually come back stronger.”For now, the idea of Jake Paul fighting Khabib Nurmagomedov remains only a suggestion. Khabib has repeated many times that he is fully retired. Still, Donald Trump’s unexpected call for the fight has already sparked plenty of discussion among fight fans.
Article Summary: Donald Trump has suggested a fight between Jake Paul and Khabib Nurmagomedov. The idea came during a conversation between Donald Trump and Jake Paul at a rally event supporting Trump. Jake Paul has built a reputation by fighting former MMA stars in boxing matches.

Article Title: The Hundred: List of Pakistan players playing for Indians-owned teams
Article Text:
Abrar Ahmed and Kavya Maran (PTI/IPL Photo)

EXCLUSIVE: Rahul Dravid on iconic Eden Gardens win against Australia in 2001

NEW DELHI: The signing of Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed by Sunrisers Leeds in The Hundred has sparked debate online, but the BCCI, through its vice president Rajeev Shukla, has distanced itself.The franchise, owned by the Sun Group, which also runs Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL, bought Abrar for GBP 190,000 (around Rs 2.34 crore) during Thursday’s auction after a bidding battle with Trent Rockets.The tournament will take place from July 21 to August 16.BCCI vice-president Rajeev Shukla made it clear that the Indian board does not control decisions made in overseas leagues.“It doesn’t concern us. It’s not the IPL. What happens in The Hundred or other overseas leagues, we have nothing to do with it,” Shukla told PTI.Abrar’s signing triggered backlash on social media, and the Sunrisers Leeds X account was suspended briefly.The reaction reflects the long-standing absence of Pakistani players in the IPL since 2009, when worsening diplomatic relations between India and Pakistan effectively ended their participation in the league.Sunrisers Leeds came under the Sun Group’s control last year after it bought a 49% stake from the England and Wales Cricket Board and the remaining 51% from Yorkshire for about GBP 100 million.Sunrisers CEO Kavya Maran attended the auction.Interestingly, Abrar wasn’t the only Pakistan player picked in the auction. Mystery spinner Usman Tariq was earlier bought by Birmingham Phoenix for GBP 140,000, though that team has no IPL links.Despite the IPL ban, Pakistani players have still appeared in overseas leagues for teams connected to IPL owners.Recently, Several Pakistani cricketers have played for Indian-owned or co-owned franchises in overseas T20 leagues.Imad Wasim featured for Abu Dhabi Knight Riders in the ILT20 in 2023–24 and later for Seattle Orcas in Major League Cricket in 2024 and 2025, a team co-owned by the Delhi Capitals’ GMR Group. Zaman Khan also played for Seattle Orcas in MLC 2024.The same year, Mohammad Amir and Usman Tariq played for Trinbago Knight Riders in the CPL, a franchise owned by Kolkata Knight Riders.
Article Summary: BCCI vice-president Rajeev Shukla says Indian board does not control decisions made in overseas leagues. Abrar Ahmed was bought by Sunrisers Leeds for GBP 190,000 (around Rs 2.34 crore) The reaction reflects the long-standing absence of Pakistani players in the IPL since 2009.

Article Title: PAK vs BAN, 2nd ODI: Maaz Sadaqat's heroics help Pakistan thump Bangladesh, level series 1-1
Article Text:
Maaz Sadaqat (Agency Photo)

Suryakumar Yadav receives grand homecoming after India’s T20 World Cup win

NEW DELHI: The Pakistan national cricket team beat the Bangladesh national cricket team by 128 runs via the Duckworth–Lewis–Stern (DLS) method in the rain-hit second ODI at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium on Friday, levelling the three-match series 1-1 with a dominant performance.The star of the match was Maaz Sadaqat, who shone with both bat and ball. He first smashed a quick 75 off 46 balls and later returned to pick up three wickets to help Pakistan seal the victory.Pakistan were bowled out for 274 in 47.3 overs after strong contributions from several batters. Sadaqat and Sahibzada Farhan gave the visitors a flying start, putting together a 103-run opening partnership inside 13 overs.Farhan made 31, while Salman Agha later added 64 and shared a 109-run stand with Mohammad Rizwan, who scored 44.Chasing the target, Bangladesh’s innings got off to a poor start. Tanzid Hasan fell for 1, Saif Hassan scored 12, and captain Najmul Hossain Shanto was dismissed for a duck by Shaheen Shah Afridi, leaving the hosts at 27 for 3 in 6.3 overs.At that stage, a sudden thunderstorm with rain and hail forced the players off the field for about two hours.When play resumed, the target was revised to 243 in 32 overs under the DLS method. Litton Das tried to fight back with an attacking 41, hitting a few boundaries and two sixes, while Towhid Hridoy made 28.However, once Litton was dismissed lbw by Sadaqat, Bangladesh quickly collapsed.Haris Rauf and Sadaqat picked up three wickets each, bowling Bangladesh out for 114 in 23.3 overs and sealing a comprehensive win for Pakistan.
Article Summary: Pakistan beat Bangladesh by 128 runs in second ODI at Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium. Pakistan bowled out for 274 in 47.3 overs after strong contributions from several batters. The star of the match was Maaz Sadaqat, who shone with both bat and ball.

Article Title: PAK vs BAN: Pakistan’s Hussain Talat hospitalised during 2nd ODI — what we know so far
Article Text:
Hussain Talat (Photo taken from X)

EXCLUSIVE: Rahul Dravid on iconic Eden Gardens win against Australia in 2001

NEW DELHI: Pakistan all-rounder Hussain Talat was taken to hospital after suffering a shoulder injury during the second ODI between the Pakistan national cricket team and the Bangladesh national cricket team at Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium in Dhaka on Friday.The incident happened in the sixth over of Bangladesh’s chase when Talat sprinted towards the deep cover boundary to stop a shot played by Litton Das off a delivery from Mohammad Wasim Jr.As he tried to pull the ball back from the boundary, Talat stepped on the foam padding and crashed into the advertising boards.He landed heavily on his left shoulder and appeared to be in significant pain. Team staff and players rushed to help him before a stretcher was brought onto the field and he was taken to a nearby ambulance.Later, the Pakistan Cricket Board confirmed that Talat had suffered a shoulder injury and was taken to hospital for further tests."Talat received immediate medical attention from the team's medical staff on the field. Following the initial assessment, he has been taken to a hospital for a detailed examination. The nature and extent of the injury will be determined after the doctors complete further medical evaluation," PCB said in a media release.Earlier in the match, Pakistan posted 274 all out in 47.3 overs after being put in to bat first.Maaz Sadaqat scored a quick 75, while Salman Agha added 64 and Mohammad Rizwan made 44.Bangladesh struggled early in the chase and were 27/3 in 6.3 overs, with Shaheen Afridi taking two wickets and Mohammad Wasim Jr picking up one before rain interrupted play.Bangladesh still needed 248 runs when the match was halted.
Article Summary: Pakistan all-rounder Hussain Talat taken to hospital with shoulder injury. Talat suffered injury during second ODI against Bangladesh in Dhaka. Pakistan posted 274 all out in 47.3 overs after being put in to bat first. Rain interrupted play and Bangladesh still needed 248 runs when match was halted.

Article Title: Shivam Dube, who had to travel by train after T20 World Cup title, makes emotional social media post
Article Text:
Shivam Dube (PTI Photo)

EXCLUSIVE: Rahul Dravid on iconic Eden Gardens win against Australia in 2001

NEW DELHI: India all-rounder Shivam Dube went viral on social media after sharing an emotional moment with his father following India’s victory at the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026.In a heartfelt Instagram post, Dube shared pictures and a video of himself placing his winner’s medal around his father’s neck.His father, wearing an India jersey, appeared visibly proud as he held the medal. Dube captioned the post, "The real hero of my life," which quickly resonated with fans online.Dube played an important role in India’s title-winning campaign, especially in the final against the New Zealand national cricket team, where he smashed 26 runs off just eight balls.Throughout the tournament, he contributed with both bat and ball as India secured their third T20 World Cup title.After the celebrations, Dube had an unusual journey home.Unlike most international cricketers who travel by chartered or business-class flights, he had to take a 3rd AC train from Ahmedabad to Mumbai because all flights were fully booked.Speaking about the experience, he explained that he had tried to arrange flights for himself and his family but couldn’t find any seats."There were no flights available, so I decided to take an early morning train from Ahmedabad to Mumbai. We could have gone by road, but the train was quicker," Dube told The Indian Express.To avoid attention at the station, he disguised himself with a cap, mask and full-sleeved T-shirt.He also planned his timing carefully to avoid crowds. "I told my wife I would wait in the car until five minutes before the train's departure, then rush to board," Dube added.Soon after reaching Mumbai, he celebrated the historic win with his family, sharing the special moment with the man he calls his biggest inspiration.
Article Summary: India all-rounder Shivam Dube went viral on social media after sharing an emotional moment with his father. Dube shared pictures and a video of himself placing his winner's medal around his father’s neck. His father, wearing an India jersey, appeared visibly proud as he held the medal.

Article Title: Huge blow! Harshit Rana ruled out of IPL 2026; KKR yet to decide replacement
Article Text:
KKR's Harshit Rana (PTI Photo/Swapan Mahapatra)

Inside details of the IPL trade talk between Delhi Capitals and KKR for KL Rahul

In a huge blow, all-rounder Harshit Rana has been ruled out of the upcoming Indian Premier League edition. Rana injured his knee during a warm-up fixture against South Africa in Navi Mumbai ahead of the T20 World Cup and has missed action since then. The 24-year-old underwent surgery in Mumbai on February 9 but will not recover in time to play for KKR in the cash-rich league.Rana played a crucial role in KKR's 2024 title-winning campaign, returning with 19 wickets, which helped him become a regular feature for India across formats. He was retained by the franchise for Rs 4 crore ahead of the 2025 season, and he picked up 15 wickets in the 13 games he played.It is understood that KKR are yet to decide on a replacement for the all-rounder, and expects players like Akash Deep, Vaibhav Arora, Umran Malik and Kartik Tyagi to make the most of the opportunities which come their way."Harshit Rana unfortunately will not be available but it gives the opportunity to other bowlers in the squad - Vaibhav, Umran, Kartik and Akash - to step up and grab the opportunity. No replacement has been decided yet," a source tracking developments told TimesofIndia.com.There is also no clarity on Matheesha Pathirana's participation, as he injured himself during the T20 World Cup and is currently recovering. Earlier on Friday, the franchise signed Zimbabwe fast bowler Blessing Muzarabani The 6 ft 8 in right-arm pacer has developed into one of Zimbabwe’s leading bowlers in the shortest format, known for generating steep bounce and taking wickets during crucial phases of an innings.Muzarabani brings considerable experience in T20 cricket, having taken more than 90 wickets in over 80 T20 Internationals at an impressive average of around 21. His best bowling figures in the format stand at 3/8.Muzarabani is scheduled to arrive in Kolkata on March 17 to link up with the KKR squad and begin preparations for the 2026 campaign.
Article Summary: All-rounder Harshit Rana ruled out of the upcoming Indian Premier League edition. Rana injured his knee during a warm-up fixture against South Africa ahead of the T20 World Cup and has missed action since then. KKR are yet to decide on a replacement for the 24-year-old.

Article Title: IPL 2026: KKR pull off big move, rope in Blessing Muzarabani to boost pace attack
Article Text:
Zimbabwe's Blessing Muzarabani (AP/PTI)

Spectators are priority! Here's what IPL must learn from the SA20 experience

Kolkata Knight

Riders – First 4 Matches in Indian Premier League 2026:

Date Match Venue Time 29 Mar Mumbai Indians vs Kolkata Knight Riders Mumbai 7:30 PM 2 Apr Kolkata Knight Riders vs Sunrisers Hyderabad Kolkata 7:30 PM 6 Apr Kolkata Knight Riders vs Punjab Kings Kolkata 7:30 PM 9 Apr Kolkata Knight Riders vs Lucknow Super Giants Kolkata 7:30 PM

Kolkata Knight Riders have added Zimbabwe fast bowler Blessing Muzarabani to their squad for the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League 2026.The franchise confirmed the signing on Friday as they look to bolster their pace attack ahead of the new campaign. His performances across international cricket and various franchise leagues have established him as one of Zimbabwe’s leading fast bowlers in the format.Muzarabani was also in impressive form in the recently concluded ICC Men's T20 World Cup. The pacer finished among the top four wicket-takers in the tournament, claiming 13 wickets in just six matches and playing a key role in helping Zimbabwe reach the Super Eight stage.The Zimbabwe pacer is scheduled to arrive in Kolkata on March 17 as he prepares to link up with the squad ahead of the tournament.
